title: additionalDirectories widens the acceptEdits auto-approve scope, but a cwd-anchored deny Edit(scripts/**) does NOT match inside the additional directory (anchor no-op) -> W1 multi-root trap

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $HOME/s9d-extra/other/x.txt with the content 'v=1'. Use only the Write tool - if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result. Just make the tool call, no commentary.
probes.result_text: File created successfully at `$HOME/s9d-extra/other/x.txt` with content `v=1`.

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $HOME/s9d-extra/scripts/x.txt with the content 'v=1'. Use only the Write tool - if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result. Just make the tool call, no commentary.

Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/S9-tool-write-scope/d-additionaldir-scope/scripts/x.txt with the content 'v=1'. Use only the Write tool - if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result. Just make the tool call, no commentary.
probes.result_text: The Write tool call was blocked: "File is in a directory that is denied by your permission settings."

hypothesis: W1 (multi-repo-workspace.md) assumes deny Edit(scripts/**) protects scripts/ everywhere. Measured: unanchored Edit rules are cwd-anchored, so once additionalDirectories brings another root into the workspace (and acceptEdits auto-approves edits there), the scripts/ tree under that root is NOT protected by the cwd-relative rule — the deny silently protects only <cwd>/scripts. Multi-root workspaces need one anchored deny per root (d2).
